verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B (ATP synthase F1 beta subunit) is canonically located in the mitochondrial inner membrane matrix-facing F1 complex. However, it is one of several F1-ATPase subunits documented to appear on the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane on tumor cells and activated immune cells (ecto-ATP synthase / ecto-F1-ATPase). On tumor cells, this ecto-form has been shown to function as a receptor for angiostatin and apolipoprotein A-I, and has been targeted therapeutically. This cancer/activation-state surface display qualifies as cell_state_induced contextual surface accessibility.
